<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>
<tr>
<td width="36%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:36.5%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 20.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Indiana </font></p>    </td>
<td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="10%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#151;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="10%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#151;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="10%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4</font></p>    </td>
<td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="10%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4</font></p>    </td>
<td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="10%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.0%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>  </table>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We ceased operations of the Sonora Regional Cancer Center as of June, 2008. This new center that we developed in a joint venture commenced operation on April&nbsp;6, 2005. Our initial contribution to the joint venture which managed this treatment center was $2.1&nbsp;million. The closure was the result of the expiration of the lease agreement for the building space housing this treatment center. In connection with the closure, </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">we impaired the value of Sonora&#146;s clinical medical equipment to estimated net realizable value based on market price for similar assets and recognized an impairment loss from the discontinued operation for the property, plant, and equipment in our consolidated statement of operations of approximately $0.3 million, $0 million, and $2.4 million ($0.5 million of which was attributable to the joint venture partner), for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2010, 2009 and 2008, respectively. We also recorded an impairment loss for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2008 from the discontinued operation of approximately $3.0 million representing the reduction in value of goodwill allocable to Sonora. The impairment charges were recorded net of the $0.8 million of related income tax benefit.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In 2004, we entered into a contract to operate a treatment center on behalf of a third party payor for which we provided equipment and services. The center was closed in June&nbsp;2009 as a result of the expiration of our contract to operate this treatment center. We recognized $0.6&nbsp;million of closure costs during 2009.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Since the beginning of 2007 we have acquired 11 treatment centers for approximately $66.6&nbsp;million plus transaction expenses.&#160; On July&nbsp;1, 2008, the Company acquired the assets of a radiation treatment center in Indiana previously held by ROA Associates,&nbsp;LLP.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">34<a name="PB_34_030136_7748"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='34',FILE='C:\JMS\105569\11-7643-1\task4553596\7643-1-bi.htm',USER='105569',CD='Mar 15 20:42 2011' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The operations of the foregoing acquisitions have been included in the accompanying consolidated statements of income from the respective dates of each acquisition. When we acquire a treatment center, the purchase price is allocated to the assets acquired and liabilities assumed based upon their respective values on the acquisition date. The excess of the purchase price over the fair value of net assets acquired is allocated to goodwill. We believe the fair values assigned to the assets acquired and liabilities assumed were based on reasonable assumptions. We do not currently have any binding commitments or agreements to make any acquisitions.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Third-Party Contracting</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our affiliated physician groups receive payments for their services and treatments rendered to patients covered by third-party payors. Most of our affiliated physician groups&#146; revenue from third-party payors is from managed care organizations and is attributable to contracts we have negotiated with them. We believe that the scale of our treatment center network improves our ability to negotiate more attractive agreements with these payors. These agreements specify fixed fees for services provided at our treatment centers, and give the managed care organization the ability to market access to our affiliated physician groups and physicians to their members. This is a benefit to the managed care organization, and also gives our affiliated physician groups access to a larger pool of potential patients.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Receivables Management</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our affiliated physician groups provide radiation therapy services under a significant number of different professional and technical codes, which determine reimbursement. Our affiliated physician groups rely on us to provide the complex coding, billing and collections process. Fees billed to contracted third-party payors and government sponsored programs are automatically adjusted to the allowable payment amount at the time of billing. For third-party payors with whom we do not have contracts and self-pay patients, the amount we expect will be paid for services is estimated and recorded at the time of billing. We revise these estimates at the time billings are collected for any actual differences in the amount received and the net billings due.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our billing office staff expedites the payment process using a number of methods, including electronic inquiries, phone calls and automated letters, which helps to facilitate timely payment by the relevant payor. We routinely prepare aging reports for accounts receivable by date of billing. Our collection team then utilizes these reports to assess and determine the payors requiring additional focus and collection efforts. Our accounts receivable exposure on Medicare, Medicaid and third-party payor balances are largely limited to contractual adjustments. Our exposure to bad debts on balances relating to these types of payors historically has been de minimis.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In the event of denial of payment, we follow the payor&#146;s standard appeals process, both to secure payment and to negotiate with the payors, as appropriate, to modify their medical policies to expand coverage for the newer and more advanced treatment services that we provide. If all reasonable collection efforts with these payors have been exhausted by our central billing office staff, the account receivable is written off to the contractual allowance.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Sources of our Affiliated Physician Groups&#146; Net Revenue By Payor</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our affiliated physician groups&#146; net revenue is summarized by payor source in the following table:</font></p>

<td width="14%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.98%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.78%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">%</font></p>    </td>
<td width="15%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:15.0%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.76%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">%</font></p>    </td>
<td width="15%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:15.0%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.5%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">%</font></p>    </td>   </tr>  </table>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our affiliated physician groups receive payments for their services and treatments rendered to patients covered by Medicare, Medicaid, third-party payors and self-pay. Generally, our affiliated physician groups&#146; net revenue is impacted by a number of factors, including the payor mix, the number and nature of procedures performed and the rate of payment for the procedures.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Third-Party Payors.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Third-party payors include private health insurance as well as related payments for co-insurance and co-payments. Most of our affiliated physician groups&#146; third-party payor revenue is attributable to contracts</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">35<a name="PB_35_030437_7608"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='35',FILE='C:\JMS\105948\11-7643-1\task4551704\7643-1-bk.htm',USER='105948',CD='Mar 15 00:06 2011' -->

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">where a set fee is negotiated relative to services provided by our treatment centers. We do not have any contracts that individually represent over 5% of our affiliated physician groups&#146; net revenue. Although the terms and conditions of our managed care contracts vary, they are typically for terms of less than five years and provide for automatic renewals. If payments by managed care organizations and other third-party payors decrease then our net revenue and net income could decrease.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Medicare and Medicaid.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Since cancer disproportionately affects elderly people, a significant portion of our affiliated physician groups&#146; net revenue is derived from the Medicare program as well as related co-payments. Medicare reimbursement rates are determined by the Center for Medicare and Medicaid Services, or CMS, and are typically lower than our rates to third-party payors and self-pay patients. Further, Medicaid reimbursement rates are typically lower than Medicare rates. Government sponsored programs generally reimburse on a fee-for-service basis based on a predetermined reimbursement rate schedule. Medicare reimbursement rates are determined by a formula that typically changes on an annual basis. We depend on payments from government sources and any changes in Medicare or Medicaid programs could result in a decrease in our net revenue and net income.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Self-Pay.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Self-pay consists of payments for treatments by patients not otherwise covered by Medicare, Medicaid and third-party payors.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Seasonality</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our results of operations historically have fluctuated on a quarterly basis and can be expected to continue to fluctuate. Some of the patients of our Florida treatment centers are part-time residents in Florida during the winter months. Hence, these treatment centers have historically experienced higher utilization rates during the winter months than during the remainder of the year. In addition, referrals are typically lower in the summer months due to traditional vacation periods.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Comparison of the Years Ended December&nbsp;31, 2009 and 2010</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Net revenue.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Net revenue for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 was $99.8&nbsp;million compared to $106.8&nbsp;million for the same period in 2009, a decrease of $7.0&nbsp;million or 6.6%. The year-over-year decline in net revenue was principally due to (1)&nbsp;a $2.2&nbsp;million decrease resulting from the permanent closure of one center in June&nbsp;2009 due to the expiration of a contract to operate the center and the resulting loss of revenue from patient treatments performed at that center, (2)&nbsp;a $1.1&nbsp;million decrease due to the replacement of linacs at two single-linac treatment centers, which caused a temporary closure of one treatment center and reduced IMRT utilization at the second treatment center and (3)&nbsp;a 6% decrease in patient treatments as a result of broad economic factors. Due to the location of one of the single-linac treatment centers, we were unable to transfer patients from the temporarily closed center to any of our other treatment centers in the network. Both treatment centers returned to normal operations by during the third quarter of 2010.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Salaries and benefits.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Salaries and benefits for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 decreased 2.8% to $34.7&nbsp;million from $35.7&nbsp;million for the same period in 2009. The decrease was primarily due to a reduction in personnel and related salary and employee benefit costs during the period as a result of a decrease in patient treatments, partially offset by a management retention payment of $0.5&nbsp;million and $0.5&nbsp;million of other compensation payments approved by the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Depreciation and amortization.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Depreciation and amortization expense for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 decreased slightly to $18.4&nbsp;million compared to $18.7&nbsp;million for the same period of 2009.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">General and administrative expenses.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; General and administrative expenses for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 increased $5.4&nbsp;million to $37.5&nbsp;million, or 16.8%, from $32.1&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2009. The increase was due to $1.7&nbsp;million in costs associated with the operation of a temporary vault while replacing a linac at a single-linac treatment center, and $0.9 million in costs related to initial public filings, offset by a decrease of $2.1&nbsp;million in center and corporate overhead costs. Reimbursement of legal expenses and proceeds related to the settlement of litigation of $4.3 million offset general and administrative expenses incurred during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2009.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Interest expense.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Interest expense increased $6.2&nbsp;million to $22.9&nbsp;million or 37.1% for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 compared to $16.7&nbsp;million for the same period in 2009. The increase in interest expense was principally due to the refinancing of debt and the issuance of $210.0&nbsp;million of Senior Secured Notes which bear interest at higher rates than the retired debt.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">36<a name="PB_36_030437_8146"></a></font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Debt extinguishment cost.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; The Company recognized $2.9&nbsp;million of debt extinguishment cost in the second quarter of 2010 in connection with the refinancing.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Income tax (expense) benefit.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Income tax benefit increased $8.7&nbsp;million to $7.0&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 compared to income tax expense of $1.7&nbsp;million during the same period in 2009. The increase was due to a $17.4&nbsp;million loss from continuing operations before income taxes for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 compared to income from continuing operations before income taxes of $3.0&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2009.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Comparison of the Years Ended December&nbsp;31, 2008 and 2009</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Net revenue.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Net revenue decreased by $1.9&nbsp;million, or 1.8%, from $108.7&nbsp;million in 2008 to $106.8&nbsp;million in 2009. The decrease was primarily due to lower reimbursement rates from CMS. CMS reimbursement rate reduction decreased net revenue by approximately $1.9&nbsp;million, or 1.5%, with an additional decrease in net revenue due to a 4.4% decrease in total treatment center census. This decrease to net revenue was partially offset by a 3.1% increase in IMRT and 17.2% increase in IGRT treatment procedures, which are billable at a higher rate than CBT treatments.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Salaries and benefits.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Salaries and benefits decreased by $2.2&nbsp;million, or 5.8%, from $37.9&nbsp;million in 2008 to $35.7&nbsp;million in 2009. Salaries and benefits as a percentage of net revenue decreased from 34.9% for 2008 to 33.5% for 2009. The decrease was primarily due to reduced salaries and benefits expenses related to staff reductions and elimination of bonuses in 2009.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Depreciation and amortization.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Depreciation and amortization expense increased by $0.6&nbsp;million, or 3.4%, from $18.1&nbsp;million in 2008 to $18.7&nbsp;million in 2009. This increase was primarily due to depreciation related to new equipment installed at certain treatment center locations at the end of 2008.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">General and administrative expenses.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; General and administrative expenses decreased by $7.6&nbsp;million, or 19.0%, from $39.7&nbsp;million in 2008 to $32.1&nbsp;million in 2009. General and administrative expenses as a percentage of net revenue decreased from 36.5% for 2008 to 30.1% for 2009. The decrease was primarily due to a decrease in legal and accounting expenses of approximately $6.6&nbsp;million as a result of legal expenses incurred in 2008 which were partially reimbursed to us by one of our physician groups and in connection with the settlement of litigation related to those legal activities, the proceeds from which reduced legal expenses in 2009, and a decrease in corporate relocation expenses of $1.1&nbsp;million resulting from moving the corporate headquarters from California to Colorado in 2008.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Interest expense.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Interest expense decreased by $1.5&nbsp;million, or 8.4%, from $18.3&nbsp;million in 2008 to $16.7&nbsp;million in 2009. The decrease in interest expense was principally due to a decrease in interest rates and the average balance of debt outstanding.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Loss on interest rate swap.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Loss on interest rate swap decreased by $2.5&nbsp;million, or 72.8%, from $3.4&nbsp;million in 2008 to $0.9&nbsp;million in 2009. The decrease in the loss on interest rate swap was principally due to a continued but less significant decline in interest rate expectations in 2009 compared to 2008.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Equity interest in net earnings of joint venture.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Equity interest in earnings of joint venture decreased by $0.4&nbsp;million, or 35.5%, from $1.1&nbsp;million in 2008 to $0.7&nbsp;million in 2009. The decrease in the equity interest in earnings of joint venture was principally due to a decline in joint venture revenue and the resultant decline in net earnings.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Discontinued operations.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We incurred an impairment loss resulting from discontinued operations in 2008 of $4.1&nbsp;million. The loss was the result of the closure of operations of our Sonora Regional Cancer Center, or Sonora, in which we had a 50% interest in a joint venture as of June&nbsp;30, 2008. We recognized an impairment loss from the discontinued operation for the intangible assets and property, plant, and equipment of approximately $2.4&nbsp;million ($0.5&nbsp;million of which was attributable to the Sonora non-controlling joint venture partner) in 2008. We also recorded an impairment loss from discontinued operations of approximately $3.0&nbsp;million representing the reduction in value of goodwill allocable to Sonora. The impairment charges were recorded net of the $0.8&nbsp;million of related income tax benefit in 2008.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Income tax (expense) benefit.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Income tax expense increased $4.6&nbsp;million from an income tax benefit of $3.0&nbsp;million in 2008 to $1.7&nbsp;million of income tax expense in 2009. The increase was primarily due to income from continuing operations of $3.0&nbsp;million before income taxes in 2009 compared to a loss of $7.3&nbsp;million from continuing operations before income taxes in 2008.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">37<a name="PB_37_030437_7091"></a></font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Liquidity and Capital Resources</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On May&nbsp;13, 2010, we concluded the offering of the Senior Secured Notes. Proceeds from the sale of the Senior Secured Notes were used primarily to repay our then existing senior credit facility and subordinated debt. Concurrently with the closing of the offering, our direct wholly-owned subsidiary, Oncure Medical, and each of its direct and indirect subsidiaries entered into a new senior secured revolving credit facility with GE Capital Markets,&nbsp;Inc., as sole lead arranger and book manager, General Electric Capital Corporation, as administrative agent and collateral agent, and the other lenders from time to time party thereto.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The new revolving senior credit facility provides for aggregate commitments of up to $40.0&nbsp;million as of May&nbsp;13, 2010, including a letter of credit sub-facility of $2.0&nbsp;million and a swing line sub-facility of $2.0&nbsp;million, and provides for the increase, at our option, of aggregate commitments by $10.0&nbsp;million, subject to certain conditions. The new revolving credit facility is undrawn as of December&nbsp;31, 2010 and expires in May&nbsp;2015.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our primary ongoing liquidity requirements are for working capital, debt service, capital expenditures and acquisitions. We finance these liquidity requirements through a combination of cash on hand, cash flows from operating activities and the incurrence of additional indebtedness, including borrowings under our revolving credit facility.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2010, we had total cash and cash equivalents of $7.0&nbsp;million, $206.6&nbsp;million of outstanding long-term indebtedness, net of discount, and availability under our revolving credit facility of up to $40.0&nbsp;million, which may be increased pursuant to the terms of the indenture governing the notes and the agreement governing our revolving credit facility.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Based on our current business plan, we believe that our existing cash balances, cash generated from operations and availability under our revolving credit facility will be sufficient to meet our anticipated cash needs for at least the next 12&nbsp;months. However, our future cash requirements could be higher than we currently expect as a result of various factors. Our ability to meet our liquidity needs could be adversely affected if we suffer adverse results of operations, or if we violate the covenants and restrictions to which we are subject under our revolving credit facility. Additionally, our ability to generate sufficient cash from our operating activities is subject to general economic, political, regulatory, financial, competitive and other factors beyond our control. Our business may not generate sufficient cash flow from operations, and future borrowings may not be available to us under our revolving credit facility in an amount sufficient to enable us to pay our service or repay our indebtedness or to fund our other liquidity needs, and we may be required to seek additional financing through credit facilities with other lenders or institutions or seek additional capital through private placements or public offerings of equity or debt securities. No assurances can be given that we will be able to complete additional debt or equity financings on terms favorable to us or at all.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Cash Flows Provided By Operating Activities</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash provided by operating activities for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2008, 2009 and 2010 was $10.7&nbsp;million, $16.9&nbsp;million and $10.1&nbsp;million, respectively.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash provided by operating activities decreased $6.8&nbsp;million to $10.1&nbsp;million in the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 compared to $16.9&nbsp;million in the same period in 2009. The decrease was primarily a result of a decrease in net income in 2010 of $12.1&nbsp;million, a decrease in deferred income tax expense of $8.3&nbsp;million and an increase in cash utilized by other liabilities of $2.6&nbsp;million primarily due to settlement of our interest swap agreement, offset by an increase in cash provided by accrued expenses of $5.0&nbsp;million primarily due to accrued interest, debt extinguishment costs of $2.9&nbsp;million, an increase in cash provided by accounts receivable of $4.1&nbsp;million and a decrease in cash utilized for prepaid expenses and other assets of $3.2&nbsp;million.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash provided by operating activities increased by $6.2&nbsp;million from $10.7&nbsp;million in 2008 to $16.9&nbsp;million in 2009. This increase was primarily attributable to a $9.7&nbsp;million increase in net income offset in part by $4.1&nbsp;million loss from discontinued operations.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Cash Flows Used In Investing Activities</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash used in investing activities for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2008, 2009 and 2010 was $59.3&nbsp;million, $6.0&nbsp;million and $7.3&nbsp;million, respectively.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">38<a name="PB_38_030437_455"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='38',FILE='C:\JMS\105948\11-7643-1\task4551704\7643-1-bk.htm',USER='105948',CD='Mar 15 00:06 2011' -->

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash used in investing activities increased by $1.3&nbsp;million to $7.3&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010 from $6.0&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2009. The increase was primarily due to an increase in capital expenditures of $0.9 million and a decrease in distributions received from unconsolidated joint ventures of $0.5 million.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Historically, our capital expenditures have been primarily for equipment and information technology software systems and equipment. Total capital expenditures (excluding acquisitions) were $14.2&nbsp;million, $7.2&nbsp;million and $8.0&nbsp;million in 2008, 2009 and 2010, respectively. Capital expenditures for 2011, exclusive of the purchase of radiation oncology treatment centers, are expected to be approximately $7.8&nbsp;million primarily related to equipment purchases and maintenance capital. To the extent that we acquire or internally develop new radiation oncology treatment centers, we may need to increase our expected capital expenditures.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Cash Provided By (Used In) Financing Activities</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:39.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash provided by financing activities for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2008 was $56.9&nbsp;million. Net cash used in financing activities for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2009 and 2010 was $14.9&nbsp;million and $1.1 million, respectively.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash used in financing activities decreased by $13.8&nbsp;million from $14.9&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2009, to $1.1&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2010. The decrease was primarily due to $206.3&nbsp;million of proceeds from the issuance of Senior Secured Notes in May&nbsp;2010, offset by $198.5&nbsp;million in debt repayments and related loan costs of $9.0&nbsp;million and a decrease in net payments on the line of credit of $12.0&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2009.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash used in investing activities decreased by $53.3 million from $59.3 million used in investing activities in 2008 to $6.0 million used in investing activities in 2009.&#160; This decrease was primarily attributable to $46.5 million in cash paid for acquisitions in 2008 compared to no acquisitions in 2009 and a $7.0 million reduction in property and equipment purchases in 2009 as compared to 2008.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net cash used in financing activities increased by $71.8&nbsp;million from $56.9&nbsp;million provided by financing activities in 2008 to $14.9&nbsp;million used in financing activities in 2009. The increase was primarily attributable to $47.2&nbsp;million in proceeds from the issuance of debt and a $12.0&nbsp;million draw on our line of credit in 2008 compared to the repayment of $12.0&nbsp;million on our existing line of credit and no new borrowings or issuance of debt in 2009.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On May&nbsp;13, 2010, we concluded an offering for $210.0&nbsp;million of 11.75% Senior Secured Notes (&#147;Senior Notes&#148;) which will mature on May&nbsp;15, 2017. The offering of the Senior Secured Notes closed on May&nbsp;13, 2010 with generated net proceeds to us of $206.3&nbsp;million which was utilized to repay the outstanding balances of our Senior term loan and Subordinated debt along with $9.0&nbsp;million of expenses of the offering. The Senior Notes are secured by the assets of the Company and our subsidiaries</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> and are guaranteed by our subsidiaries</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">. Prior to May&nbsp;15, 2013, up to 35% of the Senior Notes are redeemable at the option of the Company with proceeds from an equity offering at a redemption price of 111.75%.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Concurrently with the closing of the offering of the Senior Notes, our direct wholly-owned subsidiary, Oncure Medical Corp. and each of its direct and indirect subsidiaries entered into a new senior secured revolving credit facility (&#147;Revolving Credit Facility&#148;) with GE Capital Markets,&nbsp;Inc., as sole lead arranger and book manager, General Electric Capital Corporation, as administrative agent and collateral agent, and the other lenders from time to time party thereto. The new Revolving Credit Facility provided for aggregate commitments of up to $40.0&nbsp;million, including a letter of credit sub-facility of $2.0&nbsp;million and a swing line sub-facility of $2.0&nbsp;million, and will provide for the increase, at our option, of aggregate commitments by $10.0&nbsp;million, subject to certain conditions. The Revolving Credit Facility bears interest at a rate of Prime plus 3.5% (6.75% at December&nbsp;31, 2010) or LIBOR plus 4.5% (4.76% at December&nbsp;31, 2010). The revolving line of credit is subject to an unused line fee of 0.75% to be paid quarterly.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Discussion of Non-GAAP Information</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Adjusted EBITDA consists of net income as adjusted for depreciation and amortization, interest expense, interest and other income, income taxes, income from discontinued operations, non-cash equity based compensation expense, impairment loss resulting from discontinued operations, loss on interest rate swap, the management fee that we pay to Genstar and for certain other items that we believe are appropriate to manage the business and for the understanding of the reader, as detailed below. You are encouraged to evaluate each adjustment and the reasons we consider it appropriate for supplemental analysis.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We present Adjusted EBITDA because we consider it to be an important supplemental measure of our performance and believe this measure is frequently used by securities analysts, investors and other interested parties in the evaluation of companies in our industries with similar capital structures. We believe issuers of &#147;high yield&#148; securities also present Adjusted EBITDA because investors, analysts and rating agencies consider it useful in measuring the ability of those issuers to meet debt service obligations. We believe that Adjusted EBITDA is an appropriate supplemental measure of debt service capacity, because cash expenditures for interest are, by definition, available to pay interest, and income tax expense is inversely correlated to interest expense because income tax expense goes down as deductible interest expense goes up and depreciation and amortization are non-cash charges.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Adjusted EBITDA has limitations as an analytical tool, and you should not consider this item in isolation, or as a substitute for an analysis of our results as reported under GAAP. Some of these limitations are:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">excludes certain income tax payments that may represent a reduction in cash available to us;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">does not reflect our cash expenditures, or future requirements, for capital expenditures or contractual commitments;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">does not reflect changes in, or cash requirements for, our working capital needs; and</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">does not reflect the significant interest expense, or the cash requirements necessary to service interest or principal payments on our debt, including the notes;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">39<a name="PB_39_030437_4141"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='39',FILE='C:\JMS\105948\11-7643-1\task4551704\7643-1-bk.htm',USER='105948',CD='Mar 15 00:06 2011' -->

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">although depreciation and amortization are non-cash charges, the assets being depreciated and amortized will often have to be replaced in the future, and Adjusted EBITDA does not reflect any cash requirements for such replacements;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">is adjusted for all non-cash income or expense items that are reflected in our statements of cash flows;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">other companies in our industry may calculate Adjusted EBITDA differently than we do, limiting its usefulness as a comparative measure; and</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">we include certain adjustments that may be recurring in nature and may not meet the GAAP definition of infrequent or unusual items, but we believe these items are appropriate to manage the business and for the understanding of the reader.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Because of these limitations, Adjusted EBITDA should not be considered as a measure of discretionary cash available to us to invest in the growth of our business. We compensate for these limitations by relying primarily on our GAAP results and using Adjusted EBITDA only supplementally.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In calculating Adjusted EBITDA, we make certain adjustments that are based on assumptions and estimates. In addition, in evaluating Adjusted EBITDA, you should be aware that in the future we may incur expenses, or realize benefits, similar to those adjusted in this presentation. We calculate Adjusted EBITDA in accordance with the debt covenants of our revolving credit agreement and certain adjustments are subject to debt administrator concurrence.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Adjusted EBITDA is a supplemental measure of our performance and our ability to service debt that is not required by, or presented in accordance with, GAAP. Adjusted EBITDA is not a measurement of our financial performance under GAAP and should not be considered as an alternative to net income or any other performance measures derived in accordance with GAAP, or as an alternative to cash flow from operating activities as measures of our liquidity. In addition, our measurements of Adjusted EBITDA may not be comparable to similarly titled measures of other companies.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following table reconciles net income to Adjusted EBITDA for the periods presented:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="80%" style="border-collapse:collapse;margin-left:.75in;width:80.0%;">
<tr>
<td width="51%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:51.86%;">

<td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.22%;">

<tr>
<td width="51%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:51.86%;">

<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.12%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>    </td>
<td width="12%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.5%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">2008</font></b></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.12%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>    </td>
<td width="12%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.5%;">

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="1" width="25%" noshade color="black" align="left"></div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(a)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Loss on interest rate swap that did not qualify for hedge accounting. See Note&nbsp;6 of the Notes to our Consolidated Financial Statements included elsewhere in this annual report.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(b)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Represents legal expenses and proceeds received related to the settlement of litigation.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(c)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Includes $0.3&nbsp;million and $0.7&nbsp;million related to one-time acquisition-related audit expenses incurred during 2008 and 2009, respectively; and $0.3&nbsp;million and $0.3 million in acquisition-related expenses in 2008 and 2010, respectively.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(d)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Represents one-time costs associated with the relocation of our corporate headquarters to Colorado.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(e)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Represents management fees paid to Genstar.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(f)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Represents severance costs related to general personnel reductions during 2008 and 2009; and the departure of our former CEO and other workforce reduction costs during 2010.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(g)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Includes estimated legal costs related to a lease settlement and temporary vault costs during permanent vault construction at one center location.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(h)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Includes $0.5&nbsp;million of one-time lease-termination costs in 2009, deferred rent amortization of $0.5&nbsp;million, $0.3&nbsp;million and $0.3 million in 2008, 2009 and 2010, respectively; other expenses related to discontinued operations of $0.1&nbsp;million in 2008; $0.5&nbsp;million conditional management retention expense in 2010; $0.2&nbsp;million of costs related to re-organizing physician groups in Florida into a consolidated billing entity in 2010; and $0.9 million of professional fees associated with the registration of our Senior Secured Notes in 2010.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Contractual Obligations</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="1" width="25%" noshade color="black" align="left"></div>
</div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(1)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Interest payments on long-term debt are based on the fixed annual interest rate of 11.75%.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In addition to the obligations in the table above, we have an advisory services agreement with Genstar Capital, LLC. Pursuant to this agreement, we pay Genstar an annual management fee of $1.5&nbsp;million for financial and advisory services. This agreement continues from year to year unless otherwise amended or terminated.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Additionally, in January&nbsp;2006, we formed the Vidalia Regional Cancer Center,&nbsp;LLC as a joint venture with Meadows Regional Medical Center to develop and operate a new treatment center in Vidalia, Georgia. Both we and Meadows Regional Medical Center have committed to fund an initial $1.0&nbsp;million of initial capital upon the successful issuance of a Georgia CON by the Georgia Department of Community. Although the CON has been issued and there has been no development activity to date, it is reasonably possible that development activities will commence within the next year.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Off Balance Sheet Arrangements</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We do not currently have any off-balance sheet arrangements with unconsolidated entities or financial partnerships, such as entities often referred to as structured finance or special purpose entities, which would have been established for the purpose of facilitating off-balance sheet arrangements or other contractually narrow or limited purposes. As such, we are not materially exposed to any financing, liquidity, market or credit risk that could arise if we had engaged in these relationships.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Inflation</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We are impacted by rising costs for certain inflation-sensitive operating expenses such as equipment, labor and employee benefits. We believe that inflation has not had a material impact on us, but may in the future.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">41<a name="PB_41_030437_7906"></a></font></p>
